zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Saye於大約1182年出生在英國英格蘭賀福爾利哈達斯 卡素時，她的父親Hugh de Saye Baron of Burford23歲，母親Mabel Marmion24歲。她於1209年之前與Robert de Mortimer在英國英格蘭賀福爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。她去世於大約1242年9月在英國英格蘭諾福爾可格雷哈爾享年61歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Saye于大约1182年出生在英国英格兰賀福爾利哈達斯 卡素时，她的父亲Hugh de Saye Baron of Burford23岁，母亲Mabel Marmion24岁。她于1209年之前与Robert de Mortimer在英国英格兰賀福爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。她去世于大约1242年9月在英国英格兰諾福爾可格雷哈爾享年61岁。

nameMeanings.name: Saye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Slovenian Saje: nickname for someone with very dark, sooty hair, derived from the root of sajast ‘sooty’, or directly from saja ‘soot’.
West African (Liberia): from the personal name Saye, found among the Mano (Mah) people, denoting a first-born boy.

variants.name: Say
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Sai in Orne or Say in Indre, France, perhaps so called from a Gaulish personal name Saius + the Latin locative suffix -acum (see also French name 3 below).

English: nickname, perhaps for someone who made or sold say, a type of woollen cloth, or for an habitual wearer of clothes made of this material, from Middle English sai(e) ‘say’ (Old French saie, from Latin saga, plural of sagum ‘military cloak’). See also Sayer .

French (southern): topographic name from saix (from Latin saxum) ‘rock’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example, Say in Loire, Saix in Tarn and Vienne, Le Saix in Hautes-Alpes, or Les Saix in Isère.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Saye was born about 1182, in Richard's Castle, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Hugh de Saye Baron of Burford, was 23 and her mother, Mabel Marmion, was 24. She married Robert de Mortimer before 1209, in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. She died about September 1242, in Gressenhall, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Saye est née environ 1182, à Richard's Castle, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Hugh de Saye Baron of Burford, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Mabel Marmion, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Robert de Mortimer avant 1209, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Elle est décédée environ septembre 1242, à Gressenhall, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 61 ans.

Sie hat vor 1209 in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom Robert de Mortimer geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Saye(이)가 대략 1182년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 리처드'스 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hugh de Saye Baron of Burford(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mabel Marmion(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1209년 이전 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르에서 Robert de Mortimer(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1242년 9월 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 그레센홀에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Saye родилась около 1182 г. в местности ричард'с кастл, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Hugh de Saye Baron of Burford было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Mabel Marmion, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert de Mortimer перед 1209 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Она умерла около сентябрь 1242 г. в местности грессенолл, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 61 год.
